item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My gaming group is currently running an old-school DnD module focused on a certain infamous severed hand. Therefore, my headcanon for this will now always be:

Vecna's Vainglorious Digit

Right-hand, right-brain; so it embodies all his intelligence score power. Like wizard magic and mental domination. Also, super evilness.
Left-hand, left-brain; so this just gets the lich's weaker wisdom score attributes. Mostly failings such as vanity, pride, and a rather paltry ability to manipulate others via appeals to emotion. Which of course means a personality that's less arch-villain and more the kind of jerk who enjoys animal cruelty.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hm… I wonder if the ring of dogs was responsible for the heightened flow rate…

Regardless, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I see your point. The intent was the bottom sentence. I'll change it to that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea - one turn of phrase bugged me, though:
"trace amounts of SCP-1789 began to undergo necrosis"

To me, this suggests that necrosis only affects samples taken from 1789, or the necrosis is visible only via molecular analysis. Was the intent something like this:

"SCP-1789 began to exhibit superficial necrosis"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not to mention, whenever one cult/sect/pharoa would come into power, a common tactic would be to rewrite things so that the prior group's/person's favored god wasn't so great.. That's how you end up with stories where, say, a god who was once depicted as the right-hand adviser to Ra is now suddenly just a schlub pulling a chariot for other gods..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So…

which finger is it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP gets me
its so damn weird.
Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like that, it makes their religions seem more like stories with different editors changing it as they go along.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It…sorta is.

I started out with the severed body part of a God.

Then I was like, "wait shit, that's pretty much Osiris isn't it?" so I threw in all the Egyptian stuff.

But of course, you're free to interpret it however the hell you want.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are several mutually exclusive versions of the myth out there. Some of it is differences in translation, but mostly it just seems that people didn't demand that their mythology be internally consistent back in the day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought it was eaten by a fish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Don't worry, his mom sewed it back on before he married his sister.
…
The ancient gods had a rather different set of family values.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm fairly certain the part Osiris is missing is NOT his finger…………

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A three foot finger of a king. Nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
is that the straight up canon intent because if so

awesome

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If that's just Osiris' finger, I'd hate to see the rest of him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much better, glad to see this cleaned up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
We have a giant finger that moves and secretes drugs. But the most interesting part of this object is that it's sapient.

Interviews. We need them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The sheer level of "what" in this, not to mention the laughs, and the fact that I just earlier today read the Honey Mummy one, makes me +1 this through mental compulsion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Three-foot-long finger that oozes goop: good idea. Maybe Dave lost it.

My problems come with the compulsions and the way the hallucinations are reported. To the latter, it's put awfully sparse and vaguely. To the former, it seems tacked on. I mean, you're missing the point that this is a three-foot-long, talking finger that bleeds LSD. That by itself should really be enough. Make an interview log or something; this is very plain and definitely in need of something else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this one when it was first posted and I still like it now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weird enough and executed well enough to earn my upvote. Good work.
